title: euphrasia gracilis subsp. tetraquetra
intro: Euphrasia gracilis subsp. tetraquetra, also known as tetraquetra euphrasia or eyebright, is a subspecies of perennial plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating eye problems, such as conjunctivitis, and other conditions like sore throats and respiratory issues. Euphrasia gracilis subsp. tetraquetra is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of iritis, conjunctivitis, and other eye infections, as well as soothe sore throats and coughs. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support the health of the eyes and respiratory system.
